item.title: RMM Abuse Explodes as Hackers Ditch Malware
item.description:
Remote monitoring and management (RMM) software offers hackers multiple benefits, including stealth, persistence, and operational efficiency.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Poland Energy Survives Attack on Wind, Solar Infrastructure
item.description:
Russia-aligned groups are probable culprits behind the wiper attacks against renewable energy farms, a manufacturer, and a heating and power plant.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Embeds Malware in Android Devices
item.description:
Keenadu downloads payloads that hijack browser searches, commit ad fraud, and execute other actions without user knowledge.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Singapore & Its 4 Major Telcos Fend Off Chinese Hackers
item.description:
After detecting a zero-day attack, the country's effective response was attributed to the tight relationship between its government and private industry.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: A CISO's Playbook for Defending Data Assets Against AI Scraping
item.description:
Discover a strategic approach to govern scraping risks, balance security with business growth, and safeguard intellectual capital from automated data harvesting.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Threat Intelligence Has a Human-Shaped Blind Spot
item.description:
How I realized what I was taught to about threat intelligence was missing something crucial.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Grandstream VoIP Bug Highlights SMB Security Blind Spot
item.description:
CVE-2026-2329 allows unauthenticated root-level access to SMB phone infrastructure, so attackers can intercept calls, commit toll fraud, and impersonate users.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Connected and Compromised: When IoT Devices Turn Into Threats
item.description:
Reused passwords, a lack of network segmentation, and poor sanitization processes make the Internet of Things' attack surfaces more dangerous.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Abu Dhabi Finance Week Exposed VIP Passport Details
item.description:
Unprotected cloud data sends the wrong signal at a time when the emirate's trying to attract investors and establish itself as a global financial center.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
item.description:
A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
item.description:
The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA: Hackers Exploiting Vulnerability in Product of Taiwan Security Firm TeamT5
item.description:
The vulnerability added to CISA’s KEV catalog affects ThreatSonar Anti-Ransomware and it was patched in 2024. The post CISA: Hackers Exploiting Vulnerability in Product of Taiwan Security Firm TeamT5 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Vulnerabilities in Popular PDF Platforms Allowed Account Takeover, Data Exfiltration
item.description:
Novee researchers discovered 16 vulnerabilities in Foxit and Apryse PDF tools that could have been exploited via malicious documents or URLs. The post Vulnerabilities in Popular PDF Platforms Allowed Account Takeover, Data Exfiltration appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cogent Security Raises $42 Million for AI-Driven Vulnerability Management
item.description:
The Series A funding round, led by Bain Capital, brings the total raised by Cogent to $53 million. The post Cogent Security Raises $42 Million for AI-Driven Vulnerability Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New Keenadu Android Malware Found on Thousands of Devices
item.description:
The malware has been preinstalled on many devices but it has also been distributed through Google Play and other app stores. The post New Keenadu Android Malware Found on Thousands of Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: German Rail Giant Deutsche Bahn Hit by Large-Scale DDoS Attack
item.description:
The cyberattack disrupted information and booking systems and lasted for several hours. The post German Rail Giant Deutsche Bahn Hit by Large-Scale DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OpenClaw Security Issues Continue as SecureClaw Open Source Tool Debuts
item.description:
OpenClaw faces security vulnerabilities and misconfiguration risks despite rapid patches and its transition to an OpenAI-backed foundation. The post OpenClaw Security Issues Continue as SecureClaw Open Source Tool Debuts appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ivanti Exploitation Surges as Zero-Day Attacks Traced Back to July 2025
item.description:
Security researchers have seen the vulnerabilities being exploited to deliver shells, conduct reconnaissance, and download malware. The post Ivanti Exploitation Surges as Zero-Day Attacks Traced Back to July 2025 appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Venice Security Emerges From Stealth With $33M Funding for Privileged Access Management
item.description:
Formerly named Valkyrie, the company’s funding includes $25 million raised in a Series A round. The post Venice Security Emerges From Stealth With $33M Funding for Privileged Access Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nearly 1 Million User Records Compromised in Figure Data Breach
item.description:
The blockchain-based lender has confirmed a data breach after ShinyHunters leaked over 2GB of data allegedly stolen from the company. The post Nearly 1 Million User Records Compromised in Figure Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: French Government Says 1.2 Million Bank Accounts Exposed in Breach
item.description:
The Ministry of Economy reported discovering unauthorized access to the national bank account registry FICOBA. The post French Government Says 1.2 Million Bank Accounts Exposed in Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Researchers warn Volt Typhoon still embedded in US utilities and some breaches may never be found
item.description:
Dragos's Rob Lee said Volt Typhoon is "still very active, and they're still absolutely mapping out and getting into embedding in U.S. infrastructure, as well as across our allies.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Leading Japanese semiconductor supplier responding to ransomware attack
item.description:
The company said it detected unusual activity within its IT environment on Sunday and activated incident response protocols and isolated the impacted systems.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dismantling Defenses: Trump 2.0 Cyber Year in Review
item.description:
The Trump administration has pursued a staggering range of policy pivots this past year that threaten to weaken the nation’s ability and willingness to address a broad spectrum of technology challenges, from cybersecurity and privacy to countering disinformation, fraud and corruption. These shifts, along with the president’s efforts to restrict free speech and freedom of the press, have come at such a rapid clip that many readers probably aren’t even aware of them all.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
item.description:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Security Lapse at MYH: Private Agency Fined After Viral Video
item.description:
Devdiscourse reports: A private security firm faced a fine of Rs 25,000 [USD $275.77] on Tuesday after a viral video allegedly revealed neglect, as two women carried a patient on a stretcher out of Government Maharaja Yashwantrao Chikitsalaya (MYH), officials stated. Considered one of the largest state-operated hospitals in Madhya Pradesh, MYH is under the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hackers Offer to Sell Millions of Eurail User Records
item.description:
Eduard Kovacs reports: The Netherlands-based company disclosed a data breach in mid-January, informing the public that the personal, order, and travel reservation information of customers who were issued a Eurail pass may have been compromised. Those who reserved a seat through Eurail may also be affected. Eurail said at the time that hackers accessed systems storing basic... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Oklahoma Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes say ransomware disrupted tribal systems
item.description:
Dysruption Hub reports: The Cheyenne and Arapaho Tribes of Oklahoma say a ransomware attack forced them to shut down tribal computer networks, disrupting email and phone service and temporarily suspending some operations as systems are restored and investigators work the case. Tribal Gov. Reggie Wassana said in a statement that the tribes were “targeted by a ransomware... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Nigerian Man Sentenced to Eight Years in Prison for Computer Intrusion and Theft
item.description:
Defendant conspired to use stolen taxpayer information to file over 1,000 fraudulent tax returns seeking millions of dollars in tax refunds BOSTON – A Nigerian national living in Mexico, who was extradited to the United States, was sentenced yesterday in federal court in Boston for his role in a scheme to break into Massachusetts tax... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Data Protection Failures on Moldovan Portals Leave Citizens at Risk
item.description:
Breaches involving government entities may be politically motivated, such as the 2022 compromise of the Presidency of Moldova’s email server or the 2024 compromise of Moldova’s parliamentary email servers just days before the country’s presidential election. Other incidents may be due to human error or may be financially motivated. In Part 1, DataBreaches describes a... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Leaked Data Raises Questions About Hackers’ Claims and Moldova’s Prior Denial
item.description:
Data leaked on a dark website, allegedly from a Moldovan portal, does not support the hackers’ claims about how they obtained it. It also raises questions about the government’s May 2025 claim that its network had not been compromised. In Part 1, DataBreaches described a data exposure incident involving Moldova’s job applicant portal, cariere.gov[.]md. In... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: University of Mississippi Medical Center closes all clinics in wake of cyberattack
item.description:
Dysruption Hub reports what sounds like a very serious cyberattack affecting the University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC): University of Mississippi Medical Center said a cybersecurity attack knocked multiple IT systems offline Thursday, cutting off access to its Epic electronic medical records platform and prompting the Jackson-based system to close clinics and cancel outpatient care.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Privacy breaches following the Lapu Lapu Day Festival
item.description:
A report issued by the Office of the Information and Privacy Commissioner for British Columbia states: Between April 30, 2025 and June 20, 2025 the OIPC received breach notifications from the Vancouver Coastal Health Authority (VCH), the Fraser Health Authority (FHA), Providence Health Care (PHC), and the Provincial Health Services Authority (PHSA) for privacy breaches... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Meriden, Connecticut shuts down city internet after disruption attempt
item.description:
Dysruption Hub is all over the breach news today. They report: Meriden, Connecticut, took city internet services and public Wi-Fi offline after officials reported an attempted disruption, limiting some municipal operations as police investigate. The city said emergency services were not affected. City officials disclosed the issue Feb. 17, saying the IT department responded immediately after identifying... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: HHS OCR Settles HIPAA Security Rule Investigation with Top of the World Ranch Treatment Center
item.description:
Settlement Marks OCR’s 11th Enforcement Action in OCR’s Risk Analysis Initiative Today, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services’ (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) announced a settlement with Top of the World Ranch Treatment Center (TWRTC), a substance use disorder treatment provider in Illinois, for a potential violation of the Health Insurance Portability... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Do your Staff need Training on HIPAA in Emergency Situations?
item.description:
Emergencies in healthcare are not limited to extreme weather, wildfires, or other natural disasters. Today’s most disruptive incidents are just […] The post Do your Staff need Training on HIPAA in Emergency Situations? appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Carespring Health Care Management & LifeBridge Health Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
item.description:
Carespring Health Care Management in Ohio and LifeBridge Health in Maryland have agreed to settle class action lawsuits stemming from […] The post Carespring Health Care Management & LifeBridge Health Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: March 2, 2025: Deadline for Electronic Submission of 2025 Workplace Injury and Illness Data
item.description:
The deadline for submitting electronic workplace injury and illness information to the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) is March […] The post March 2, 2025: Deadline for Electronic Submission of 2025 Workplace Injury and Illness Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: FREE Webinar Today: How to Complete Your Annual HIPAA Risk Assessment
item.description:
In 2025, 95% of OCR HIPAA fines cited missing or deficient HIPAA risk assessments as a core basis for enforcement. […] The post FREE Webinar Today: How to Complete Your Annual HIPAA Risk Assessment appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Managed Care Advisors / Sedgwick Notify Patients of Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Managed Care Advisors and Sedgwick Government Solutions recently announced a cybersecurity incident involving unauthorized access to a corporate Secure File […] The post Managed Care Advisors / Sedgwick Notify Patients of Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Academic Urology & Urogynecology of Arizona Data Breach Affects 73K Patients
item.description:
Academic Urology & Urogynecology of Arizona, a division of Palo Verde Hematology and Oncology that serves patients throughout Arizona, has […] The post Academic Urology & Urogynecology of Arizona Data Breach Affects 73K Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: March 1, 2026: Small Healthcare Data Breach HIPAA Reporting Deadline
item.description:
Healthcare data breaches discovered in calendar year 2025 that affected fewer than 500 individuals must be reported to the HHS’ […] The post March 1, 2026: Small Healthcare Data Breach HIPAA Reporting Deadline appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Three Healthcare Providers Affected by Ransomware Attacks
item.description:
Issaqueena Pediatric Dentistry in South Carolina, Enhabit Home Health & Hospice in Texas, and AltaMed Health Services in California have […] The post Three Healthcare Providers Affected by Ransomware Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Shows Elevenfold Increase in Data-only Extortion Attacks
item.description:
There has been a sharp increase in data-only extortion incidents, with ransomware gangs increasingly opting not to encrypt files, instead […] The post Data Shows Elevenfold Increase in Data-only Extortion Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data breach at fintech firm Figure affects nearly 1 million accounts
item.description:
Hackers have stolen the personal and contact information of nearly 1 million accounts after breaching the systems of Figure Technology Solutions, a self-described blockchain-native financial technology company. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers target Microsoft Entra accounts in device code vishing attacks
item.description:
Threat actors are targeting technology, manufacturing, and financial organizations in campaigns that combine device code phishing and voice phishing (vishing) to abuse the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization flow and compromise Microsoft Entra accounts. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Texas sues TP-Link over Chinese hacking risks, user deception
item.description:
Texas sued networking giant TP-Link Systems, accusing the company of deceptively marketing its routers as secure while allowing Chinese state-backed hackers to exploit firmware vulnerabilities and access users' devices. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Nigerian man gets eight years in prison for hacking tax firms
item.description:
A Nigerian national was sentenced to eight years in prison for hacking multiple tax preparation firms in Massachusetts and filing fraudulent tax returns seeking over $8.1 million in refunds. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News
